properties: Modern Java web app threat model — built live via the tm CLI.
Username + plaintext password supplied at sign-in.
OIDC ID token after a successful auth challenge.
Rendered receipt or notification email body.
Serialized session blob keyed by session id.
Untrusted public network — any client can originate traffic here.
Customer browsing from a regular browser.
Internet-facing edge of the VPC where TLS terminates and the ALB lives.
Routes to ECS services by host/path.
Edge cache and TLS termination for static assets and API routes.
Application tier — runs ECS Fargate and the in-VPC cache.
Fargate cluster hosting the four Spring Boot services.
Serves the Vue SPA, proxies auth + API.
TLS terminates at the ALB, so the ALB→Frontend hop is plaintext HTTP inside the VPC.
Polls SQS for async jobs (email, billing reconciliation).
Every inbound request carries a JWT minted by the Auth Service.
Outbound AWS calls use the task IAM role, not static keys.
Fronts Cognito, mints short-lived JWTs and runs password flows.
Network-isolated durable storage subnet, no direct internet egress.
Primary relational store: accounts, orders, audit trail.
Regional AWS services (S3, SQS, SES, Cognito) reached over HTTPS with IAM auth.
Our IdP — user pools and federated sign-in.
Async work handed off by the REST API.
